---
title: "Phylo plots"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
```

## 1. Load packages and set working directory

```{r libraries}
library(ggtree)
library(ggmsa)
library(ggplot2)
library(treeio)
library(aplot)
library(dplyr)
library(patchwork)
library(Biostrings)

setwd("~/Documents/plot_phylo")

```

## 2. Load input data

```{r load-data}
#File paths
newick_file = "rodents.nh"               # Full rodent tree
newick_file2 = "rodents_noamericans.nh"   # Tree excluding American species
helder_file = "HelderPhenotypes.csv"     # 4-level phenotype classification
helder_file2 = "HelderPhenotypes2.csv"    # 3-level phenotype classification (no "red")

# BED file with alignment window coordinates and conservation scores
BMP6 = read.table(
  "~/Documents/plot_phylo/BMP6_clean.bed",
  quote = "\"", comment.char = ""
)

# One FASTA alignment per genomic window
dirs = list.files("fasta", full.names = TRUE)

# Trees
tree = read.tree(newick_file)
tree2 = read.tree(newick_file2)

# Phenotype tables
pheno = read.csv(helder_file,  header = FALSE, col.names = c("species", "phenotype"))
pheno2 = read.csv(helder_file2, header = FALSE, col.names = c("species", "phenotype"))

pheno$phenotype = factor(pheno$phenotype,  levels = c("grey", "green", "orange", "red"))
pheno2$phenotype = factor(pheno2$phenotype, levels = c("grey", "green", "orange"))

rownames(pheno) = pheno$species
rownames(pheno2) = pheno2$species
```

## 3. Color and label scheme for Helder phenotypes

```{r colors}
# Base scheme (4 categories)
helder_colors = c(
  grey = "grey",
  green = "forestgreen",
  orange = "orange",
  red = "red3"
)

helder_labels = c(
  grey = "Brachydont",
  green = "Murine",
  orange = "Hypsodont",
  red = "Hypselodont"
)

helder_colors2 = helder_colors[c("grey", "green", "orange")]
helder_labels2 = helder_labels[c("grey", "green", "orange")]
```

## 4. Tree-plotting function

```{r tree-function}
plot_helder_tree = function(tree, pheno_data, colors, labels, tiplab_size = 3) {
  ggtree(tree, layout = "rectangular", size = 1) %<+% pheno_data +
    aes(color = phenotype) +
    geom_tiplab(size = tiplab_size, linetype = 10) +
    geom_tippoint(size = 1.5) +
    geom_treescale(x = 0.5, y = -1, width = 0.05) +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = colors,
      name = "Helder Phenotype",
      labels = labels,
      na.value = "grey60"
    ) +
    theme_tree2() +
    theme(legend.position = "left", plot.margin = margin(10, 2, 10, 10))
}
```

## 5. Generating three trees

```{r trees, fig.width=8, fig.height=10}
# Full tree, 4-category phenotype
helder_tree = plot_helder_tree(tree, pheno, helder_colors, helder_labels, tiplab_size = 5)
helder_tree

# Full tree, 3-category phenotype, larger tip labels
helder_tree2 = plot_helder_tree(tree, pheno2, helder_colors2, helder_labels2, tiplab_size = 5)
helder_tree2

# Tree without American species, 4-category phenotype
helder_tree3 = plot_helder_tree(tree2, pheno, helder_colors, helder_labels, tiplab_size = 5)
helder_tree3
```

## 6. MSA window-plotting function

```{r msa-function}
# Plot a multiple sequence alignment in fixed-width windows, highlighting the
# Mus musculus row, and stack the windows vertically.

plot_msa_windows = function(filepath, aln_len, mus_index, score_label, window_size = 60) {

  starts = seq(1, aln_len, by = window_size)
  plots  = list()

  for (j in seq_along(starts)) {
    s = starts[j]
    e = min(s + window_size - 1, aln_len)
    show_leg = (j == length(starts))

    plots[[j]] = ggmsa(
      msa = filepath,
      color = "Chemistry_NT",
      font = NULL,
      show.legend = show_leg,
      start = s,
      end = e
    ) +
      annotate("rect",
               xmin = s - 0.5, xmax = e + 0.5,
               ymin = mus_index - 0.5, ymax = mus_index + 0.5,
               fill = "white", alpha = 0.25,
               color = "black", linewidth = 0.3
      ) +
      scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(s, e), labels = c(s, e)) +
      theme(
        axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = margin(1, 5, 1, 2),
        legend.position = if (show_leg) "bottom" else "none",
        legend.text = element_text(size = 7),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 8, face = "bold")
      )
  }

  wrap_plots(plots, ncol = 1) +
    plot_annotation(
      title = paste0(basename(filepath), "\n", score_label),
      theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))
    )
}
```

## 7. Build an MSA plot for every window

```{r msa-loop}
MSA_WINDOW_SIZE = 100

n_files = length(dirs)
p_msa = vector("list", n_files)
score_values = numeric(n_files)
aln_lengths = numeric(n_files)

for (i in seq_along(dirs)) {
  filepath = dirs[i]
  base = basename(filepath)

  # Extract "chrN_start_end" from the filename to look up its score
  coords = regmatches(base, regexpr("(chr[0-9XY]+)_(\\d+)_(\\d+)", base))
  hit = if (length(coords) > 0) bed_scores %>% filter(key == coords) else data.frame()

  score_label = if (nrow(hit) > 0) {
    paste0("score: ", round(hit$V4[1], 4), " | ", round(hit$V5[1], 4))
  } else {
    "score: N/A"
  }
  score_values[i] = if (nrow(hit) > 0) hit$V4[1] else NA

  seqs = readDNAStringSet(filepath)
  aln_len = unique(width(seqs))
  aln_lengths[i] = aln_len
  mus_index = which(grepl("Mus_musculus", names(seqs), ignore.case = TRUE))

  p_msa[[i]] = plot_msa_windows(
    filepath = filepath,
    aln_len = aln_len,
    mus_index = mus_index,
    score_label = score_label,
    window_size = MSA_WINDOW_SIZE
  )

  message("Plot created: ", base, " - ", score_label)
}
```

## 8. Export each MSA plot to PDF

```{r export-pdf}
for (i in seq_along(p_msa)) {
  out_name = paste0("msa_", tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(dirs[i])), ".pdf")

  pdf(
    out_name,
    width  = 14,
    height = 3 * ceiling(aln_lengths[i] / MSA_WINDOW_SIZE)
  )
  print(paste("Created",out_name))
  print(p_msa[[i]])
  dev.off()
}
```

## 9. Highlight the lowest- and highest-scoring windows

```{r minmax-mosaic, fig.width=14, fig.height=8}
idx_max = which.max(score_values)
idx_min = which.min(score_values)

mosaic_minmax = wrap_plots(
  list(p_msa[[idx_min]], p_msa[[idx_max]]),
  ncol = 2, nrow = 1
) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") +
  plot_annotation(
    title = paste0(
      "Min: ", round(score_values[idx_min], 4), " (", basename(dirs[idx_min]), ")",
      "   |   Max: ", round(score_values[idx_max], 4), " (", basename(dirs[idx_max]), ")"
    ),
    theme = theme(
      plot.title      = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    )
  )

mosaic_minmax
```
